instruction: Prepare a citizen_application document with clear headings, comparative sections, and verification notes for NyayaSetu in hi.
input:
Complaint facts: शिकायतकर्ता सीमा वर्मा ने बताया कि पड़ोसी ने घर में घुसकर धमकी दी और गाली-गलौज की। घटना 5 मार्च 2026 को शाम 6 बजे हुई। गवाह के रूप में एक पड़ोसी मौजूद था।

Comparative sections to stay grounded in: BNS Section 351 - Criminal intimidation; BNSS Section 173 - Information in cognizable cases; IPC Section 506 - Criminal intimidation; CrPC Section 154 - Information in cognizable cases.

Return a clean, human-readable FIR workflow document and do not invent facts.
output:
प्रति
थाना प्रभारी अधिकारी
पुलिस स्टेशन: [पुलिस स्टेशन का नाम]

विषय: धमकी और घर में घुसकर डराने के संबंध में शिकायत आवेदन

महोदय/महोदया,

मैं सीमा वर्मा यह निवेदन करती हूँ कि दिनांक 5 मार्च 2026 को शाम 6 बजे मेरे पड़ोसी ने मेरे घर में घुसकर मुझे धमकी दी और गाली-गलौज की। घटना के समय एक पड़ोसी गवाह के रूप में मौजूद था। कृपया मेरी शिकायत दर्ज कर उचित कानूनी कार्रवाई की जाए।

समीक्षा हेतु तुलनात्मक धाराएँ:
- BNS Section 351 - Criminal intimidation
- BNSS Section 173 - Information in cognizable cases
- IPC Section 506 - Criminal intimidation

भवदीया,
सीमा वर्मा

instruction: Prepare a lawyer_analysis document with clear headings, comparative sections, and verification notes for NyayaSetu in en.
input:
Complaint facts: A draft FIR alleges online payment fraud after the complainant shared an OTP with a caller claiming to be from the bank. The complainant preserved transaction IDs, screenshots, bank alerts, and call records.

Comparative sections to stay grounded in: BNS Section 318 - Cheating; BNSS Section 173 - Information in cognizable cases; IPC Section 420 - Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property; CrPC Section 154 - Information in cognizable cases.

Return a clean, human-readable FIR workflow document and do not invent facts.
output:
Lawyer FIR Review Note

Facts summary: The draft describes alleged online payment fraud after the complainant disclosed an OTP to a caller claiming to be from the bank. The available material includes transaction IDs, screenshots, bank alerts, and call records.

Comparative sections to review:
- BNS Section 318 - Cheating
- BNSS Section 173 - Information in cognizable cases
- IPC Section 420 - Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property
- CrPC Section 154 - Information in cognizable cases

Key review points:
- Verify whether the FIR draft clearly states the fraudulent inducement and the resulting wrongful loss.
- Ensure the timeline of the call, OTP disclosure, debit alert, and bank complaint is internally consistent.
- Check whether the digital evidence is described with enough detail for later proof and recovery steps.
- Confirm whether any additional IT Act or banking-fraud context should be added on the present facts.

instruction: Rewrite the same lawyer_analysis output in a polished, submission-ready way for NyayaSetu.
input:
Complaint facts: A police FIR draft based on a handwritten complaint mentions hurt and intimidation but does not clearly mention medical records or the identity proof of the complainant.

Comparative sections to stay grounded in: BNS Section 115 - Voluntarily causing hurt; BNS Section 351 - Criminal intimidation; BNSS Section 173 - Information in cognizable cases; IPC Section 323 - Voluntarily causing hurt; IPC Section 506 - Criminal intimidation; CrPC Section 154 - Information in cognizable cases.

Return a clean, human-readable FIR workflow document and do not invent facts.
output:
Lawyer FIR Review Note

The draft FIR identifies possible allegations of hurt and criminal intimidation, but the record still appears incomplete.

Comparative sections:
- BNS Section 115 - Voluntarily causing hurt
- BNS Section 351 - Criminal intimidation
- BNSS Section 173 - Information in cognizable cases
- IPC Section 323 - Voluntarily causing hurt
- IPC Section 506 - Criminal intimidation

Key gaps to address:
- Add medical treatment details or injury description if hurt is being alleged.
- Confirm the identity proof and contact details of the complainant.
- Clarify whether the intimidation was verbal, written, digital, or accompanied by any weapon.
- Preserve witness names, timestamps, and supporting documents before the FIR is finalized.

instruction: An OCR pipeline extracted this complaint. Draft a police_fir document for NyayaSetu in en.
input:
Complaint facts: A citizen application alleges criminal intimidation and unlawful entry by a neighbour on 5 March 2026 at 6 PM. One neighbour is named as a witness.

Comparative sections to stay grounded in: BNS Section 351 - Criminal intimidation; BNS Section 329 - Criminal trespass; BNSS Section 173 - Information in cognizable cases; IPC Section 506 - Criminal intimidation; IPC Section 447 - Criminal trespass; CrPC Section 154 - Information in cognizable cases.

Return a clean, human-readable FIR workflow document and do not invent facts.
output:
Police FIR Draft

The written complaint alleges that on 5 March 2026 at about 6 PM, the accused neighbour entered the complainant's premises and issued threats. One neighbour is stated to be an eyewitness.

Suggested sections for review:
- BNS Section 351 - Criminal intimidation
- BNS Section 329 - Criminal trespass
- BNSS Section 173 - Information in cognizable cases
- IPC Section 506 - Criminal intimidation
- IPC Section 447 - Criminal trespass
- CrPC Section 154 - Information in cognizable cases

Police review should verify the place of occurrence, the exact threatening words, witness particulars, and any prior dispute history before final registration.

instruction: Rewrite the same police_fir output in a polished, submission-ready way for NyayaSetu.
input:
Complaint facts: A handwritten citizen application states that an unknown person stole a phone from the complainant near Hazratganj Market on 12 March 2026 at 7:30 PM. The complaint includes invoice details and a CCTV lead.

Comparative sections to stay grounded in: BNS Section 303 - Theft; BNSS Section 173 - Information in cognizable cases; IPC Section 379 - Theft; CrPC Section 154 - Information in cognizable cases.

Return a clean, human-readable FIR workflow document and do not invent facts.
output:
Police FIR Draft

Complainant: Rahul Sharma
Police Station: Hazratganj Police Station
Date / Time of occurrence: 12 March 2026 / 7:30 PM
Place of occurrence: Hazratganj Market, Lucknow

Gist of complaint: On the basis of the written application submitted by Rahul Sharma, it is alleged that an unknown person committed theft of the complainant's mobile phone near Hazratganj Market. The complainant has referred to the purchase invoice and possible CCTV footage from the nearby area.

Suggested comparative sections:
- BNS Section 303 - Theft
- BNSS Section 173 - Information in cognizable cases
- IPC Section 379 - Theft
- CrPC Section 154 - Information in cognizable cases

The complaint, identity details, device particulars, and available CCTV lead should be verified before final registration.

instruction: Rewrite the same citizen_application output in a polished, submission-ready way for NyayaSetu.
input:
Complaint facts: Complainant Rahul Sharma reports that his mobile phone was stolen near Hazratganj Market on 12 March 2026 at 7:30 PM. The accused is unknown. Evidence includes the purchase invoice and a request for nearby CCTV footage.

Comparative sections to stay grounded in: BNS Section 303 - Theft; BNSS Section 173 - Information in cognizable cases; IPC Section 379 - Theft; CrPC Section 154 - Information in cognizable cases.

Return a clean, human-readable FIR workflow document and do not invent facts.
output:
To
Station House Officer
Police Station: Hazratganj Police Station

Subject: Application requesting registration of complaint regarding theft of mobile phone

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, Rahul Sharma, resident of Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh, respectfully submit that on 12 March 2026 at about 7:30 PM near Hazratganj Market, my mobile phone was stolen by an unknown person. I request that my complaint be recorded and appropriate action be taken. The purchase invoice is available and CCTV footage from the nearby market area may assist the investigation.

Suggested sections for review:
- BNS Section 303 - Theft
- BNSS Section 173 - Information in cognizable cases
- IPC Section 379 - Theft

Kindly acknowledge this application and proceed according to law.

Signature
Rahul Sharma

input:
Complaint facts: A police FIR draft based on a handwritten complaint mentions hurt and intimidation but does not clearly mention medical records or the identity proof of the complainant.

Comparative sections to stay grounded in: BNS Section 115 - Voluntarily causing hurt; BNS Section 351 - Criminal intimidation; BNSS Section 173 - Information in cognizable cases; IPC Section 323 - Voluntarily causing hurt; IPC Section 506 - Criminal intimidation; CrPC Section 154 - Information in cognizable cases.

Return a clean, human-readable FIR workflow document and do not invent facts.
output:
Lawyer FIR Review Note

The draft FIR identifies possible allegations of hurt and criminal intimidation, but the record still appears incomplete.

Comparative sections:
- BNS Section 115 - Voluntarily causing hurt
- BNS Section 351 - Criminal intimidation
- BNSS Section 173 - Information in cognizable cases
- IPC Section 323 - Voluntarily causing hurt
- IPC Section 506 - Criminal intimidation

Key gaps to address:
- Add medical treatment details or injury description if hurt is being alleged.
- Confirm the identity proof and contact details of the complainant.
- Clarify whether the intimidation was verbal, written, digital, or accompanied by any weapon.
- Preserve witness names, timestamps, and supporting documents before the FIR is finalized.
